A) One male gamete fuses with nucleus of
egg cell
B) Syngamy results into dyad of cells
C) Second male gamete move toward polar
nuclei
D) Triple fusion results into PEN
Page No.-34, Easy
94. Triple fusion is-
A) Fusion of third male gamete with polar
nuclei
B) Fusion of three haploid cells
C) Fusion of second male gamete with egg
cell
D) Fusion of three haploid nuclei
Page No.-34, Easy
95. Which of these is correct?
A) Syngamy = Triple fusion + Double
fertilization
B) Double fertilization = Syngamy + Triple
fusion
C) Triple fusion = Double fertilization —
Syngamy
D) More than one option is correct
Page No.-34, Easy
96. Central cell after double fertilization
becomes-
A) Zygote B) PEN
C) PEC D) Embryo

antipodals
